title: ledum glandulosum var. columbianum
intro: Ledum glandulosum var. columbianum is a variety of the Ledum plant, commonly known as cotton grass, a low-growing, evergreen shrub native to North America. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating respiratory issues, such as colds and flu, due to its antiviral and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Ledum glandulosum var. columbianum include its potential to reduce inflammation, alleviate symptoms of bronchitis, and even act as an antiseptic, making it a valuable herbal remedy. Herbal preparations of the plant include infusions, tinctures, and salves, often used topically to treat skin conditions and wounds, and internally to support respiratory health.
